title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Graph Retrieval
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a history-rewrite plan and verification graph after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.

Inspect the Rewrite Verification trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.

content.text: For Rewrite Verification, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Rewrite Verification precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Matching final files proves a rewritten series preserves review meaning.

stimulus.title: Rewrite Verification case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Matching final files proves a rewritten series preserves review meaning.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference. Artifact under review: a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Rewrite Verification, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Rewrite Verification record says a history-rewrite plan and verification graph looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Rewrite Verification without its stated preconditions, omit the a history-rewrite plan and verification graph trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Rewrite Verification for a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Rebase Replay evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Rebase Replay assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Rebase Replay case

Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Rebase Replay case

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Rewrite Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
steps.why: Retrieving Rewrite Verification through the concrete a history-rewrite plan and verification graph case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
